zh.lifeSummary: Deacon James Hurst於大約1582年出生在英國。他於1608年10月4日與Gertrude Bannister在荷蘭努-拉藍德阿姆斯特爾達姆結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他於1639年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼被列為town's first tanning business. - he tanned wolf skins。他去世於1657年12月10日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼普萊姆斯享年76歲，埋葬在巴基斯坦آزاد جموں و کشمیر布利a爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Deacon James Hurst于大约1582年出生在英国。他于1608年10月4日与Gertrude Bannister在荷兰努-拉藍德阿姆斯特爾達姆结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他于1639年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼被列为town's first tanning business. - he tanned wolf skins。他去世于1657年12月10日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼普萊姆斯享年76岁，埋葬在巴基斯坦آزاد جموں و کشمیر布利a爾。

nameMeanings.name: Hurst
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): topographic name for someone who lived near a wood or wooded hill, from Middle English hirst(e), herst(e), hurst(e) (Old English hyrst) or a habitational name from any of the places so called, such as Hurst Green (in Mitton, Lancashire), Hirst (Northumberland), Hurst (Berkshire, Kent, Warwickshire), Hurstpierpoint (Sussex), or Hirst in Longwood (Yorkshire).
Irish: re-Anglicized form of de Horsaigh, the Gaelicized form of the English habitational name Horsey , established in Ireland since the 13th century.
German and Swiss German (also Hürst): topographic name from Middle High German hurst ‘woodland, thicket’; or a habitational name from a place so named in Westphalia.

variants.name: Herst
meanings: English: variant of   Hurst  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name or nickname from Polish herszt ‘ringleader, chieftain’.

variants.name: Horsey
meanings: English (southern): habitational name from Horsey Pignes (Somerset), Horse Eye (Sussex), or Horsey (Norfolk), from Old English hors ‘horse’ + ēg ‘island, low-lying land’.

variants.name: Hearst
meanings: English: variant of   Hurst  .
Jewish (American): adoption of the English surname (see 1 above) in place of some similar (like-sounding) original Ashkenazic surname such as   Hirsch  .

variants.name: Horst
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Phares, Alois, Armin, Fritz, Hans, Inge, Manfred, Monika, Reinhold.

North German and Dutch: topographic name from Middle Low German hurst, Middle Dutch horst ‘undergrowth, brushwood, wild place’. More specifically, the term was also used to denote a crow's or similar large bird's nest, a raised area in surrounding marshland, or an area of uncleared woodland, all of which meanings could have contributed to the surname. This surname is also a habitational name from any of numerous places called with this word. Compare Hurst .

History: As a name of German origin (see 1 above) the surname Horst is established among the American Mennonites. Their progenitors were the brothers Joseph, Michael, and Peter Horst, who came to PA in 1731 together with their widowed mother. Among their descendants the altered form Hurst is also found.

variants.name: Hirst
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hurst  .
German: variant of Hürst (see Hurst ).

nameMeanings.name: Deacon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English deken, dicoun ‘deacon, a priest's assistant at church services’ (Old French diacne, Old English diacon, dēacon, both ultimately from Late Latin diaconus, from Greek diakonos ‘servant’). Since deacons were in holy orders they would not be permitted to marry (with allowance for those few already married when ordained), so the surname is probably in most cases a secular nickname rather than the name of a church official, perhaps denoting a deacon's servant.
English: variant of   Dicken  .

Irish (Wexford): of English origin (see 1 and 2 above), taken to Ireland in the 17th century. When the Irish surname is not of English origin, it may be an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Deocáin ‘descendant of Deocán’, a personal name of uncertain etymology.

variants.name: Dakan
meanings: Probably an altered form of English   Deacon  .
Possibly of Dutch origin, see   Dakin  .

variants.name: Iacono
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Salvatore, Giovanni, Sal, Vito, Angelo, Carmine, Gaetano, Gennaro, Giuseppe, Silverio, Aniello, Antonio, Leonardo, Pacifico, Rodolfo, Rosario, Salvadore, Ubaldo.
Italian (southern):
from iacono, a southern dialect form of diacono ‘deacon’; see Deacon .

variants.name: Deakin
meanings: variant of   Deacon  .
variant of   Dicken  .
English:

variants.name: Degon
meanings: Mainly North American: of uncertain origin. It is found among Jews as well as gentiles. Among the latter it is generally from the name of the English county, either directly or as a transferred use of the surname, which derives from a British tribal name said to mean ‘worshippers of the god Dumnōnos’.

variants.name: Dicken
meanings: Medieval pet form of Dick (with the Old French suffix -on), still in occasional use.

variants.name: Deakins
meanings: English: variant of Deacon , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
English: variant of   Dickens  .
In some cases possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Dekens: genitivized patronymic from a pet form of Diederik (see Dederick ).

variants.name: Deane
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which has a double origin. In part it is a local name for someone who lived in a valley (Middle English dene, Old English denu) and in part an occupational name for someone who served as a dean, i.e. ecclesiastical supervisor (Latin decanus). The given name also sometimes represents Italian Dino (a short form of names such as Bernardino), as in the case of the American actor and singer Dean Martin ( 1917–95 ).

variants.name: Deakyne
meanings: Probably English and Irish: variant of   Deacon  .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.description: In 1620, after 2 rough months at sea, 102 Pilgrims landed at Plymouth Rock and established one of the very first English settlements in America.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Deacon James Hurst was born about 1582, in England. He married Gertrude Bannister on 4 October 1608, in Amsterdam, North Holland, Netherlands. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. In 1639, at the age of 60, his occupation is listed as town's first tanning business. - he tanned wolf skins in Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. He died on 10 December 1657, in Plymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 76, and was buried in Buriāl, Azad Kashmir, Pakistan.

fr.lifeSummary: Deacon James Hurst est né environ 1582, à Angleterre. Il a épousé Gertrude Bannister le 4 octobre 1608, à Amsterdam, Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. En 1639, à l’âge de 60 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est town's first tanning business. - he tanned wolf skins à Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Il est décédé le 10 décembre 1657, à Plymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 76 ans, et a été enterré à Buriāl, Azad Kashmir, Pakistan.

de.lifeSummary: Deacon James Hurst wurde um 1582 in England geboren. Er hat am 4. Oktober 1608 in Amsterdam, Nord-Holland, Niederlande Gertrude Bannister geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. 1639, als er 60 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als town's first tanning business. - he tanned wolf skins in Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America aufgeführt. Er starb am 10. Dezember 1657 im Alter von 76 Jahren in Plymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Buriāl, Azad Kashmir, Pakistan begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Deacon James Hurst è nato il circa 1582 nel England. Lui sposato Gertrude Bannister il 4 ottobre 1608, nel Amsterdam, Noord-Holland, Paesi Bassi. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Nel 1639, all'età di 60 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come town's first tanning business. - he tanned wolf skins nel Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. Morì il 10 dicembre 1657, nel Plymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 76 anni, e fu sepolto a Buriāl, Azad Kashmir, Pakistan.

ja.lifeSummary: Deacon James Hurstは約1582年にイギリスで生まれた。1608年10月4日にオランダノオルド-hオランドアムステルダムでGertrude Bannisterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。1639年，60歳の時，イギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニーでの職業はtown's first tanning business. - he tanned wolf skinsと記録されている。彼は1657年12月10日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニープリモウスで76歳で死去し，パキスタンآزاد جموں و کشمیرブリaルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Deacon James Hurst(은)는 대략 1582년 영국에서 태어났다. 그는 1608년 10월 4일 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 아스터다에서 Gertrude Bannister(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1639년, 60세의 나이로 그의 직업은 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이에서 town's first tanning business. - he tanned wolf skins이었다. 그는 1657년 12월 10일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 플림우스에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 파키스탄 آزاد جموں و کشمیر 버이a에 묻혔다.

pt.Amsterdam, North Holland, Netherlands: Amsterdam, Holanda do Norte, Países Baixos
pt.lifeSummary: Deacon James Hurst nasceu aproximadamente 1582, em Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Gertrude Bannister em 4 de outubro de 1608, em Amsterdam, Holanda do Norte, Países Baixos. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Em 1639, com 60 anos, sua profissão aparece como town's first tanning business. - he tanned wolf skins em Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. Ele faleceu em 10 de dezembro de 1657, em Plymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 76 anos, e foi sepultado em Buriāl, Azad Kashmir, Paquistão.

ru.lifeSummary: Deacon James Hurst родился около 1582 г. в местности Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Gertrude Bannister 4 октября 1608 г. в местности Амстердам, нурд-холланд, Нидерланды. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. В 1639 году, в возрасте 60 лет,  он работал по профессии town's first tanning business. - he tanned wolf skins в местности плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. Он умер 10 декабря 1657 г. в местности плимоут, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 76 лет и был похоронен в местности буриaл, آزاد جموں و کشمیر, Пакистан.

es.lifeSummary: Deacon James Hurst nació aproximadamente 1582, en Inglaterra. Se casó con Gertrude Bannister el 4 de octubre de 1608, en Amsterdam, Noord-Holland, Países Bajos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. En 1639, a la edad de 60 años, su ocupación registrada era town's first tanning business. - he tanned wolf skins en Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. Murió el 10 de diciembre de 1657, en Plymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 76 años, y fue sepultado en Buriāl, Azad Kashmir, Pakistán.
